firstSentences.Refrigerator: A refrigerator, commonly shortened to fridge, is a commercial and home appliance consisting of a thermally insulated compartment and a heat pump that transfers heat from its inside to its external environment so that its inside is cooled to a temperature below the ambient temperature of the room.
firstSentences.Religion: Religion is a range of social-cultural systems, including designated behaviors and practices, ethics, morals, beliefs, worldviews, texts, sanctified places, prophecies, or organizations, that generally relate humanity to supernatural, transcendental, and spiritual elements—although there is no scholarly consensus over what precisely constitutes a religion.
firstSentences.Rembrandt: Rembrandt Harmenszoon van Rijn was a Dutch Golden Age painter, printmaker, and draughtsman.
firstSentences.Renaissance: The Renaissance is a European period of history and cultural movement taking place between the Late Middle Ages and the Early Modern era.
firstSentences.Renewable energy: Renewable energy is energy made from renewable natural resources that are replenished on a human timescale.
firstSentences.René Descartes: René Descartes was a French philosopher, scientist, logician, and mathematician, widely considered a seminal figure in the emergence of modern philosophy and science during Renaissance era.
firstSentences.Reproduction: Reproduction is the biological process by which new individual organisms – offspring – are produced from their parent or parents.
firstSentences.Reptile: Reptiles, as commonly defined, are tetrapod vertebrate animals with an ectothermic metabolism and amniotic development.
firstSentences.Republic: A republic, based on the Latin phrase res publica, is a state in which political power rests with the public (people), typically through their representatives—in contrast to a monarchy.
firstSentences.Respiratory system: The respiratory system is a biological system consisting of specific organs and structures used for gas exchange in animals and plants.
firstSentences.Revolution: In political science, a revolution is a rapid, fundamental transformation of a society's class, state, ethnic, or religious structures.
firstSentences.Rhine: The Rhine is one of the major rivers of Europe.
firstSentences.Rice: Rice is a cereal grain and, in its domesticated form, is the staple food of over half of the world's population, particularly in Asia and Africa.
firstSentences.Richard Wagner: Wilhelm Richard Wagner was a German composer, theatre director, essayist, and conductor, best known for his operas, although his mature works are often referred to as music dramas.
firstSentences.Rio de Janeiro: Rio de Janeiro, also known simply as Rio, is the capital of the state of Rio de Janeiro.
firstSentences.River: A river is a natural stream of fresh water that flows on land or inside caves towards another body of water at a lower elevation, such as an ocean, lake, or another river.
firstSentences.Road: A road is a thoroughfare from one place to another, primarily used for movement of traffic.
firstSentences.Robot: A robot is a machine, especially one programmable via a computer, capable of automatically carrying out a complex series of actions.
firstSentences.Rock (geology): In geology, a rock is any naturally occurring solid mass or aggregate of minerals or mineraloid matter.
firstSentences.Rock music: Rock music is a genre of popular music that originated in the United States as "rock and roll" in the late 1940s and early 1950s, developing into a range of styles from the mid-1960s, primarily in the United States and United Kingdom.
firstSentences.Rocket: A rocket is an elongated flying vehicle that uses a rocket engine to accelerate without using any surrounding air.
firstSentences.Rocky Mountains: The Rocky Mountains, also known as the Rockies, are a major mountain range and the largest mountain system in North America.
firstSentences.Roman Empire: The Roman Empire was a state that controlled the Mediterranean and much of Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa during the classical period.
firstSentences.Roman Republic: The Roman Republic was the era of classical Roman civilisation beginning with the overthrow of the Roman Kingdom and ending in 27 BC with the establishment of the Roman Empire following the War of Actium.
firstSentences.Romanticism: Romanticism was an artistic and intellectual movement that originated in Europe towards the end of the 18th century.
firstSentences.Rome: Rome is the capital city and most populated comune (municipality) of Italy.
firstSentences.Rosa Parks: Rosa Louise McCauley Parks was an American civil rights activist.
firstSentences.Russia: Russia, or the Russian Federation, is a country in Eastern Europe and North Asia.
firstSentences.Rwanda: Rwanda, officially the Republic of Rwanda, is a landlocked country in East Africa.
firstSentences.Sahara: The Sahara is a desert spanning North Africa.
firstSentences.San Francisco: San Francisco, officially the City and County of San Francisco, is the fourth-most populous city in California and the 17th-most populous in the United States, with a population of 826,079 in 2025.
firstSentences.Science: Science is a systematic discipline that builds and organises knowledge in the form of testable hypotheses and predictions about the universe.
firstSentences.Scientific Revolution: The Scientific Revolution of the 16th and 17th centuries in Europe was an irreversible break with the natural philosophy that had preceded it, fundamentally changing how the natural world was investigated and understood.
firstSentences.Scientific method: The scientific method is an empirical method for acquiring knowledge through careful observation, rigorous skepticism, hypothesis testing, and experimental validation.
firstSentences.Seed: In botany, a seed is a plant structure containing an embryo and stored nutrients in a protective coat called a testa.
firstSentences.Semiconductor: A semiconductor is a material with electrical conductivity between that of a conductor and an insulator.
firstSentences.Senegal: Senegal, officially the Republic of Senegal, is the westernmost country in West Africa, situated along the Atlantic Ocean coast.
firstSentences.Seoul: Seoul, officially Seoul Special Metropolitan City, is the capital and largest city of South Korea, and the largest city in the whole of Korea.
firstSentences.Set theory: Set theory is the branch of mathematical logic that studies sets, which can be informally described as collections of objects.
firstSentences.Shanghai: Shanghai is a direct-administered municipality and the most populous urban area in China.
firstSentences.Shinto: Shinto , also called Shintoism, is the native religion and former ethnic cult of Japan.
firstSentences.Singapore: Singapore, officially the Republic of Singapore, is an island country and city-state in Southeast Asia.
firstSentences.Slavery: Slavery is the ownership of a person as property, especially in regard to their labour.
firstSentences.Sleep: Sleep is a state of reduced mental and physical activity in which consciousness is altered and certain sensory activity is inhibited.
firstSentences.Software: Software consists of computer programs that instruct the execution of a computer.
firstSentences.Solar System: The Solar System is the gravitationally bound system of the Sun and the masses that orbit it, most prominently its eight planets, of which Earth is one.
firstSentences.Sound: Sound is a phenomenon in which pressure disturbances propagate through an elastic material medium.
firstSentences.South Africa: South Africa, officially the Republic of South Africa (RSA), is the southernmost country in Africa.
firstSentences.South America: South America is a continent entirely in the Western Hemisphere and mostly in the Southern Hemisphere, with a considerably smaller portion in the Northern Hemisphere.
firstSentences.South Asia: South Asia is the southern subregion of Asia that is defined in both geographical and ethnic-cultural terms.
firstSentences.South Korea: South Korea, officially the Republic of Korea (ROK), is a country in East Asia.
firstSentences.Southern Ocean: The Southern Ocean, also known as the Antarctic Ocean, comprises the southernmost waters of the World Ocean, generally taken to be south of 60° S latitude and encircling Antarctica.
firstSentences.Space exploration: Space exploration is the physical investigation of outer space by uncrewed robotic space probes and through human spaceflight.
firstSentences.Spain: Spain, officially the Kingdom of Spain, is a country in Southern and Western Europe with territories in North Africa.
firstSentences.Species: A species is the basic unit of classification and a taxonomic rank of an organism, as well as a unit of biodiversity.
firstSentences.Spectroscopy: Spectroscopy is the field of study that measures and interprets electromagnetic spectra as it interacts with matter.
firstSentences.Sri Lanka: Sri Lanka, officially the Democratic Socialist Republic of Sri Lanka, formerly known as Ceylon, is an island country in South Asia.
firstSentences.Star: A star is a luminous spheroid of plasma held together by self-gravity.
firstSentences.Steel: Steel is an alloy of iron and carbon that demonstrates improved mechanical properties compared to the pure form of iron.
firstSentences.Stem cell: In multicellular organisms, stem cells are undifferentiated or partially differentiated cells that can change into various types of cells and proliferate indefinitely to produce more of the same stem cell.
firstSentences.Stoicism: Stoicism is a philosophical movement and practical guide to living, emphasizing daily self-discipline and moral improvement, which originated in the Hellenistic period of ancient Greece and proliferated well into the Roman Imperial period.
firstSentences.Sudan: Sudan, officially the Republic of the Sudan, is a country in Northeast Africa.
firstSentences.Sun: The Sun is the star located at the centre of the Solar System.
firstSentences.Supernova: A supernova is a powerful and luminous explosion of a star.
firstSentences.Supply and demand: In microeconomics, supply and demand is an economic model of price determination in a market.
firstSentences.Surrealism: Surrealism is an art and cultural movement that developed in Europe in the aftermath of World War I in which artists aimed to allow the unconscious mind to express itself, often resulting in the depiction of illogical or dreamlike scenes and ideas.
firstSentences.Sustainability: Sustainability or being sustainable is the ability to continue over a long period of time.
firstSentences.Sweden: Sweden, formally the Kingdom of Sweden, is a Nordic country located on the Scandinavian Peninsula in Northern Europe.
firstSentences.Swimming: Swimming is the self-propulsion of a person through water, usually for recreation, sport, exercise, or survival.
firstSentences.Switzerland: Switzerland, officially the Swiss Confederation, is a landlocked country located at the intersection of Central, Western, and Southern Europe.
firstSentences.Tax: A tax is a mandatory financial charge or levy imposed on an individual or legal entity by a governmental organization to support government spending and public expenditures collectively or to regulate economic activity through measures designed to mitigate negative externalities.
firstSentences.Technology: Technology is the application of conceptual knowledge to achieve practical goals, especially in a reproducible way.
firstSentences.Telescope: A telescope is a device used to observe distant objects by their emission, absorption, or reflection of electromagnetic radiation.
firstSentences.Thermodynamics: Thermodynamics is a branch of physics that deals with heat, work, and temperature, and their relation to energy, entropy, and the physical properties of matter and radiation.
firstSentences.Thomas Jefferson: Thomas Jefferson was a Founding Father, second vice president, and third president of the United States from 1801 to 1809.
firstSentences.Tibet: Tibet is a region in the western part of East Asia, covering much of the Tibetan Plateau.
firstSentences.Tokyo: Tokyo, officially the Tokyo Metropolis, is the capital and most populous city of Japan.
firstSentences.Topology: Topology is the branch of mathematics concerned with the properties of a geometric object that are preserved under continuous deformations, such as stretching, twisting, crumpling, and bending; that is, without closing holes, opening holes, tearing, gluing, or passing through itself.
firstSentences.Toronto: Toronto is the most populous city in Canada and the capital city of the Canadian province of Ontario.
firstSentences.Tropical cyclone: A tropical cyclone is a rapidly rotating storm system with a low-pressure area, a closed low-level atmospheric circulation, strong winds, and a spiral arrangement of thunderstorms that produce heavy rain and squalls.
firstSentences.Tuberculosis: Tuberculosis (TB), also known colloquially as the "white death", or historically as consumption, is a contagious disease usually caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis (MTB) bacteria.
firstSentences.Ultraviolet: Ultraviolet radiation is electromagnetic radiation of wavelengths of 100–400 nanometers, shorter than that of visible light, but longer than X-rays.
firstSentences.United Nations: The United Nations (UN) is a global intergovernmental organization established by the signing of the UN Charter on 26 June 1945 with the articulated mission of maintaining international peace and security, to develop friendly relations among states, to promote international cooperation, and to serve as a centre for harmonizing the actions of states in achieving those goals.
firstSentences.United States: The United States of America (USA), also known as the United States (U.
firstSentences.Urbanization: Urbanization is the population shift from rural to urban areas, the corresponding decrease in the proportion of people living in rural areas, and the ways in which societies adapt to this change.
firstSentences.Vaccine: A vaccine is a biological preparation that provides active acquired immunity to a particular infectious or malignant disease.
firstSentences.Venezuela: Venezuela, officially the Bolivarian Republic of Venezuela, is a country on the northern coast of South America, consisting of a continental landmass and various islands and islets in the Caribbean Sea.
firstSentences.Venice: Venice is a city in northeastern Italy and the capital of the region of Veneto.
firstSentences.Vietnam War: The Vietnam War was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam and South Vietnam and their allies.
firstSentences.Violin: The violin, sometimes referred to as a fiddle, is a wooden chordophone, and is the smallest, and thus highest-pitched instrument (soprano) in regular use in the violin family.
firstSentences.Virgil: Publius Vergilius Maro, usually called Virgil or Vergil in English, was an ancient Roman poet of the Augustan period.
firstSentences.Voltage: Voltage, also known as (electrical) potential difference, electric pressure, or electric tension, is the difference in electric potential between two points.
firstSentences.Voltaire: François-Marie Arouet, known by his pen name Voltaire, was a French Enlightenment writer, philosopher (philosophe), satirist, and historian.
firstSentences.Voting: Voting is the process of making collective decisions by means of submitting and then adding up individual choices.
firstSentences.War: War is an armed conflict between the armed forces of states, or between governmental forces and armed groups that are organized under a certain command structure and have the capacity to sustain military operations, or between such organized groups.
firstSentences.Warsaw: Warsaw, officially the Capital City of Warsaw, is the capital and largest city of Poland.
firstSentences.Water: Water is an inorganic compound with the chemical formula H2O.
firstSentences.Weather: Weather refers to the state of the Earth's atmosphere at a specific place and time, typically described in terms of temperature, humidity, cloud cover, and stability.
firstSentences.Wheat: Wheat is a group of wild and domesticated grasses of the genus Triticum.
firstSentences.William Shakespeare: William Shakespeare was an English playwright, poet and actor.
firstSentences.World Health Organization: The World Health Organization (WHO) is a specialized agency of the United Nations (UN) which coordinates responses to international public health issues and emergencies.
firstSentences.World War I: World War I, or the First World War, also known as the Great War, was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Central Powers.
firstSentences.X-ray: An X-ray is a form of high-energy electromagnetic radiation with a wavelength shorter than those of ultraviolet rays and longer than those of gamma rays.
firstSentences.Yoga: Yoga is a group of physical, mental, and spiritual practices or disciplines that originated with its own philosophy in ancient India, aimed at controlling body and mind to attain liberation (moksha), as practiced in the Hindu, Jain, and Buddhist traditions.
firstSentences.Zambia: Zambia, officially the Republic of Zambia, is a landlocked country in Southern Africa.
firstSentences.Zoroastrianism: Zoroastrianism, also called Mazdayasna and Behdin, is an Iranian religion centred on the Avesta and the teachings of Zarathushtra Spitama, who is more commonly referred to by the Greek translation, Zoroaster.
